beg.desc: Ruby is dynamic and OOP-focused. Output using puts. Variables don't need declarations.
beg.code:
puts "Hello, Ruby!"

name = "Alice"
age = 25
puts "#{name} is #{age}"

int.desc: Implement conditional statements using if, unless, and case blocks.
int.code:
status = "active"
case status
when "active"
  puts "Online"
else
  puts "Offline"
end

pro.desc: Use symbols as efficient immutable string identifiers.

beg.desc: Classes manage properties using attr_accessor variables.

int.desc: Pass blocks of code to methods using yield loops.
int.code:
def run_twice
  yield
  yield
end

run_twice { puts "Hello!" }

pro.desc: Create modules for namespace organization and mixin methods.
pro.code:
module Logger
  def log(msg)
    puts "[LOG] #{msg}"
  end
end

class App
  include Logger
end
pro.usecase: Adding behaviors to classes via mixins.

beg.desc: Handle errors using begin, rescue, and ensure blocks.
beg.code:
begin
  result = 10 / 0
rescue ZeroDivisionError => e
  puts "Error: #{e.message}"
end

int.desc: Declare packages and manage dependencies using Gemfiles.

pro.desc: Metaprogram classes dynamically using define_method structures.
pro.code:
class Developer
  define_method :code do |lang|
    puts "Coding in #{lang}!"
  end
end

Developer.new.code("Ruby")
